qna_pairs.q: How does locking MARB tokens work exactly? Like what's the minimum lock period and how do I start?
qna_pairs.a: You can lock MARB tokens for any duration from 1 week up to 4 years. The longer you lock, the more veMARB voting power you get. Just go to the MarbMarket app when it launches, connect your wallet, and choose how much MARB to lock and for how long.

qna_pairs.q: So projects can literally pay to get their pools chosen? Isn't that just like a regular DEX with a fancy name?
qna_pairs.a: It's different because the projects are paying YOU directly for your vote, not a centralized entity. The 'bribes' go to the veMARB lockers who vote for that pool. So it's the community deciding where liquidity should go, with protocols competing on value rather than marketing budgets.

qna_pairs.q: What if someone locks a massive amount of MARB and just dictates where all emissions go? Seems like it could be centralized anyway.
qna_pairs.a: That's where the ve(3,3) model comes in. While longer locks do give more power, the model is designed to distribute influence over time. Plus, voting happens weekly, so the 'power' can shift based on where different lockers choose to direct emissions.

qna_pairs.q: How is this different from other veDEX models like Solidly or Aerodrome? What makes the MegaETH version special?
qna_pairs.a: MarbMarket is bringing the proven veDEX model to MegaETH for the first time. We're adapting it specifically for the MegaETH ecosystem with its unique characteristics. The core mechanics are similar, but we're focused on building the first truly community-governed DEX on this chain.

qna_pairs.q: When exactly is Q2? Any more specific timeline for the launch?
qna_pairs.a: We're targeting late Q2 for the main launch. Follow @Marb_market on Twitter for exact dates and updates. We'll be sharing a detailed roadmap soon with all the key milestones leading up to launch.

qna_pairs.q: How much MARB do I need to lock to start earning voting power?
qna_pairs.a: You can start with any amount of MARB! The longer you lock it, the more veMARB you'll earn and influence you'll have. Even small lockers can participate in the governance from day one.

qna_pairs.q: What happens if I lock MARB but then change my mind? Can I unlock early?
qna_pairs.a: If you unlock your MARB before the lock period ends, you'll lose a portion of your veMARB. The penalty decreases over time, so we encourage long-term locks to maximize your influence and rewards.

qna_pairs.q: How is MegaETH different from other chains where veDEX models exist?
qna_pairs.a: MegaETH has lower fees and faster finality compared to Ethereum, making it more efficient for liquidity mining. We're the first veDEX to bring this model to MegaETH, creating a new ecosystem for fair rewards.

qna_pairs.q: What prevents a rich project from just 'bribing' the votes with huge rewards?
qna_pairs.a: While projects can offer bribes, the ve(3,3) model ensures longer-term lockers have more influence. If a project offers unsustainable bribes, the community can vote against them the following week. Long-term value always wins over short-term manipulation.

qna_pairs.q: How do I actually get MARB tokens before the launch?
qna_pairs.a: There's no presale! MARB will be fairly distributed through our launch mechanism. Follow @Marb_market on Twitter for the latest announcements on how and when you can get MARB tokens.

content:
I've been watching veDEX models for months. Most folks miss the real magic. It's not just about locking tokens. It's about creating this self-reinforcing cycle where your vote actually matters. MarbMarket is bringing this to MegaETH with no presale. Just fair launch mechanics. You lock MARB, you get veMARB, you vote on where emissions go. Projects pay bribes for those votes. The incentives flow back to you. It's a beautiful loop.

What I find fascinating is how long-term lockers get more influence. Your lock duration directly translates to power. The ve(3,3) model means everyone's incentives are aligned. No VCs. No insiders. Just the community deciding where liquidity goes.

This changes everything for liquidity mining. Instead of chasing random APYs, you get to vote on sustainable rewards. Protocols have to compete fairly. It's not about who has the biggest marketing budget. It's about who offers real value.

The real question I'm wrestling with: how do we prevent vote delegation from becoming another form of centralization? @RallyOnChain what's your take on keeping the truly decentralized spirit alive here? x.com/Marb_market
